# Chef Infra Installation and Distribution

Based on the GitHub repository content provided, **specific Linux installation and build instructions are not detailed in this overview page.**

The repository indicates that comprehensive documentation exists elsewhere:

- **Official Documentation**: The README directs users to [https://docs.chef.io/](https://docs.chef.io/) for detailed guidance
- **Learning Resources**: "For Chef Infra usage, please refer to [Learn Chef](https://learn.chef.io/), our self-paced, entirely free learning platform"

## Distribution Method

Chef is distributed as a Ruby gem. The repository shows:
- A `chef.gemspec` file (Ruby package specification)
- Badge indicating it's published on [badge.fury.io](https://badge.fury.io/rb/chef) (the Ruby Gems repository)
- The project is primarily written in Ruby (98.4% of codebase)

The source code is available directly from the GitHub repository at [https://github.com/chef/chef/tree/main](https://github.com/chef/chef/tree/main), where developers can also find build and testing documentation in the `/docs/dev` directory structure.

For actual installation steps and platform-specific Linux build procedures, you would need to consult the official Chef documentation site.

#!/bin/bash
# Extract Chef metadata for app installation verification

# Find the chef-cli binary
BINARY_PATH=$(/usr/bin/which chef-cli 2>/dev/null || echo "/usr/local/bin/chef-cli")
BINARY_NAME="chef-cli"
DISPLAY_NAME="Chef CLI"

# Get version
VERSION=$($BINARY_PATH --version 2>&1 | grep -oP 'version: \K[0-9.]+' || echo "unknown")

# Find .desktop files
DESKTOP_ENTRIES=""
if [ -f /usr/share/applications/chef-cli.desktop ]; then
  DESKTOP_ENTRIES="/usr/share/applications/chef-cli.desktop"
fi

# Find icon files
ICON_PATHS=""
if [ -d /var/lib/gems ]; then
  # Check for icons in the gem installation
  GEM_ICONS=$(find /var/lib/gems -name "*chef*" -type f 2>/dev/null | grep -i "icon\|image" | head -5)
  if [ -n "$GEM_ICONS" ]; then
    ICON_PATHS="$GEM_ICONS"
  fi
fi

# If no icons found in gems, use a generic application icon
if [ -z "$ICON_PATHS" ]; then
  if [ -f /usr/share/icons/hicolor/48x48/apps/application-x-ruby.png ]; then
    ICON_PATHS="/usr/share/icons/hicolor/48x48/apps/application-x-ruby.png"
  elif [ -f /usr/share/icons/hicolor/48x48/apps/utilities-terminal.png ]; then
    ICON_PATHS="/usr/share/icons/hicolor/48x48/apps/utilities-terminal.png"
  fi
fi

# Output JSON
cat << EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$BINARY_PATH",
  "binary_name": "$BINARY_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": "$DESKTOP_ENTRIES",
  "icon_paths": $([ -n "$ICON_PATHS" ] && echo "[\"$ICON_PATHS\"]" || echo "[]"),
  "version": "$VERSION"
}
EOF

        DESKTOP_ICON=$(grep "^Icon=" "$desktop" | cut -d'=' -f2- | head -1)
        break 2

ICON_ARRAY=()

# Strategy 1: If we found an Icon= field in .desktop file, try to resolve it
if [ -n "$DESKTOP_ICON" ]; then
  # If it's an absolute path
  if [ -f "$DESKTOP_ICON" ]; then
    ICON_ARRAY+=("$DESKTOP_ICON")
  else
    # Try to find it in icon directories
    for icon_dir in /usr/share/icons /usr/local/share/icons ~/.local/share/icons; do
      if [ -d "$icon_dir" ]; then
        icon_found=$(find "$icon_dir" -type f -name "${DESKTOP_ICON}*" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
        if [ -n "$icon_found" ]; then
          ICON_ARRAY+=("$icon_found")
          break
        fi
      fi
    done
  fi
fi

  DISPLAY_NAME=$(echo "$BINARY_NAME" | sed 's/-/ /g' | sed 's/^./\U&/g')

if [ ${#ICON_ARRAY[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
  ICON_JSON=$(printf '"%s"' "${ICON_ARRAY[@]}" | paste -sd ',' | sed 's/^/[/; s/,$/]/')

  ICON_JSON="[]"

      [ ${#ICON_ARRAY[@]} -gt 0 ] && break
    fi
  done

      if [ -n "$icon_found" ]; then

      fi
    fi
  done
